title: Executing Applications
text: Executing Applications You can execute the application using uniface.exe or make a copy of uniface.exe with your application name. If required, create an .ini file and assignment file for the application. You can use the [APPLICATION] section in the .ini file to specify a custom icon and logo for the application. Supply your application shell as a parameter when running the Uniface runtime engine. For example: uniface.exe myapp . Only uniface.exe is capable of running an application if it is given as a parameter. Alternatively, copy uniface.exe to a file with the same name as your application. For example, copy uniface.exe to myapp.exe . When myapp.exe is executed, Uniface attempts to locate an application called myapp , with a myapp.ini file and an accompanying myapp.asn file (unless otherwise specified). The myapp.exe executable cannot run another application if it is given as a parameter. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
